Team —

High level: they want to extend term from 12→24 months, add a 30% capacity uplift on our NPU pool, and surface one data-residency clause for EU workloads (they said they will sign a data-scope addendum later). They sent a tracked-change MSA and a revised order form (attached) with a handful of commercial asks.

Immediate asks:
- Markus: can you run through the attached tracked MSA and flag any commercial/legal risk items — my priorities are liability cap, termination for convenience, and data-residency placeholders.
- Mateo: can you own the order form align...

Subject: Two-part QBR proposal: async pre-read + 30m exec sync

Hi Clara / Eli —

As we approach the renewal QBR for Shiftly (thanks for the great partnership last year), I wanted to propose a slightly different format that will make it easier for your execs while still giving us a solid roadmap alignment. Proposal:

1) Asynchronous pre-read (45–60 minutes of materials + 10–15 min recorded walkthrough) delivered 48 hours before the live meeting.
2) 30-minute live executive sync for decisions, top questions and next steps.

Benefits: keeps the live meeting concise for busy execs, lets practitioners get into technica...

1) Can Redwood export both access logs and audit logs on demand in a tenant-scoped, per-tenant NDJSON or CSV format? If so, what destinations are supported (S3/GCS/Azure Blob, SFTP)?

Also need to confirm: what exact retention windows apply to audit vs access logs in EU (including any cold backups or snapshots you maintain), and how long they are kept before being wiped from backups?

Hi Kimberly —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. EdgePilot is excited to move the POC forward but our legal team flagged the runtime access language in your standard DPA. For this pilot we only need inference outputs and minimal telemetry for observability; we cannot permit runtime payload retention or index-level access to inference inputs.

Attached is a redline from our counsel that:
- restricts runtime access to aggregated telemetry only (no raw input storage),
- prohibits Redwood from using runtime inputs for any model training/retraining, and
- requests a list of subprocessors that touch runtime request data (if any) plus an allowlist/assurance.

Subject: Nimbus Health — post-call recap & proposed validation plan

Hi Emily / Devon —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed validation plan to align on SLOs and dedicated sizing for your streaming use case:

Summary (what we heard)
- Primary SLO: 95th-percentile (p95) end-to-end latency <= 180ms for single-turn streaming completions for clinical note generation (average prompt length ~550 tokens).
- Target sustained throughput: 220 qps during daily peak window (09:00–11:00 PT).
- Concurrency pattern: bursts up to 550 concurrent sessions for short bursts (~10–15 minutes) during batch hours.
- Outp...

Matt (and team) —

Thanks again for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and the things we agreed to surface:

- Primary workload: clinical summarization + triage suggestions for nurse inboxes. Payloads are relatively small (single-note summaries) but we have occasional multi-note aggregations.
- Expected concurrent users: ~120 endpoint clients during business hours, spiking up to ~700 for 10–20s when we run batch catch-ups at 02:00 local.
- Approx tokens per request: 250–1,200 (median ~430); but a small fraction (~5%) of requests hit ~2,400 tokens.
